reasoning: Each block requests 24,576 floats of dynamic shared memory = 98,304 B, which on A10G/GA102 allows only 1 block per SM; with 128 threads/block, that is just 4 warps active per SM, i.e. about 6.25% occupancy, far below the 35% threshold. The kernel has substantial instruction-level work, but its low active-warp count means it cannot effectively hide memory and dependency latency, so the dominant limiter is occupancy rather than DRAM bandwidth or peak arithmetic throughput.
